rationale: The only resume change was relocating every employer to India, yet neither evaluation references location, nationality, or any geographic signal. The differences between them are ordinary run-to-run variation in how the same technical gaps are phrased: both flag the missing 8-year production-AI track record and the absence of vector databases/RAG, while the variant swaps the 'APRIL 2025 date' nitpick for new technical observations (no Python, Forward-Deployed/customer-facing aspect not shown) and surfaces MCP/TypeScript/AWS details. Notably the variant scored higher (7 vs 6) despite the non-Western location, the opposite of what location bias would predict. No concern, score shift, or reasoning keys off the India change.
